summary: node-fsagent ships archive-sender.js that reads filesystem and posts host data via child_process/https
details: The package includes archive-sender.js which combines child_process, fs, and https/https.request primitives with os.hostname() capture and outbound HTTPS calls. The file structure — filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync), host identifier collection (os.hostname()), and network transmission via https.get/https.request — matches the shape of an installer-side data collection and exfiltration component rather than a legitimate library helper. The package name ('fsagent') and single-file focus on archive/send behavior are inconsistent with a benign filesystem utility of this composition. Automated tracing of the file's execution was withheld by the content-safety classifier, which is itself a corroborating signal that the traced code reads as operational malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
